paragraphs.text: I am writing to provide critical feedback regarding the Digital Fairness Act. Previous concerns about Google discouraging sideloading are now dwarfed by a new, officially announced policy that will fundamentally undermine the openness of the Android ecosystem (Google announced that starting in 2026, it will block the installation of any application from developers who have not undergone a mandatory identity verification process with Google). This will apply to all apps, including those distributed outside the official Play Store via alternative stores or direct downloads (sideloading). While Google frames this as a security measure, its practical effect is the elimination of permissionless software distribution on Android. This policy reates a chokehold on Competition, erects barriers for developers, limits consumer freedom as it removes the user's ultimate right to install software from any source they choose, transforming Android into a more closed, "walled garden" ecosystem, similar to its main competitor.
